name: Podium
what_it_does: Podium is a local business platform combining review generation, two-way SMS messaging, and payment collection via a unified API, primarily targeting multi-location businesses.
use_cases: Agents sending SMS review requests to customers after service completion to drive Google and Facebook reviews
Routing inbound customer messages from multiple channels (SMS, Google, Facebook) to a unified inbox for agent handling
Triggering payment collection via text message after service delivery without requiring a card-present transaction
Automating review monitoring and alerting workflows when negative reviews appear on Google or Facebook
Managing contacts and conversation history for multi-location businesses with location-aware routing
not_for: Ecommerce or DTC brands — Yotpo or Reviews.io are better for product-specific review collection
Enterprise B2B reputation management — Bazaarvoice or Trustpilot handle corporate-scale needs
Pure payment processing — Stripe or Square are more capable and cheaper for payment-only use cases
best_when: You run a multi-location service business (auto dealership, dental practice, home services) and need Google review generation, SMS communication, and text-to-pay in one platform.
avoid_when: You're an ecommerce company or need product-level review syndication — Podium is built for local service businesses, not online retail.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with scopes for partner/platform integrations. API key (Bearer token) for direct integrations. Scopes cover contacts, conversations, reviews, and payments separately. Location-level tokens required for multi-location operations.

pricing.notes: Pricing is per location and is among the pricier options in local business software. Payments and some messaging features may have per-transaction or usage fees. No free trial without sales engagement.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; standard REST API rate limits apply, estimated 100 req/min

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Podium developer docs are reasonably complete for core operations but thin on edge cases. Error responses return structured JSON with codes. Webhook documentation covers major events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Contact creation supports external ID for deduplication. Message sends are not idempotent — duplicates can occur if agents retry without checking status first.
known_agent_gotchas: Multi-location businesses require location-scoped tokens — agents must manage credentials per location
SMS message sending is subject to carrier filtering — review request messages must follow compliance guidelines
Review invitations to Google/Facebook are subject to platform policies — excessive volume can trigger flags
Payments via text require PCI-compliant handling — agents must not store card data
Rate limits are undocumented — implement conservative throttling especially for bulk operations
Annual contracts make it expensive to test integrations — development environment access requires paid plan

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. PCI-DSS compliant for payment features. TLS enforced. OAuth2 with scopes provides good access granularity. Location-level tokens limit blast radius of credential compromise.
